summary: Clean, well‑mastered soundboard recording with solid performance; standout beats on “Beat It On Down the Line”, “Ramble On Rose” and a tight “Not Fade Away → Goin’ Down the Road Feeling Bad”.
review: The newly mastered sbd capture is praised for its clarity, balanced highs and lows, and proper track ordering, though some listeners object to the separate tuning tracks. Musically the show delivers several high points—an uptempo “Beat It On Down the Line”, a tight ensemble on “Next Time You See Me”, a fiery “Ramble On Rose” and an impressive dual‑drum climax on “Not Fade Away → Goin’ Down the Road Feeling Bad”—while the first set is rated only C+. Overall the recording is listenable and the performance worthwhile for Deadheads.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality receives a modest ~3.8 from detailed fan notes, while recording quality is praised (≈5) but offset by criticism of tuning‑track handling; weighted blend yields ~3.5.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Excellent mastering with clean highs and lows, tracks correctly titled and ordered. Some listeners dislike the inclusion of separate tuning tracks, calling them clutter.

standout_songs: Beat it on Down the Line
Not Fade Away > Goin' Down the Road Feeling Bad
show_quality.setlist_flow: A 16‑song first set (graded C+) followed by a concise one‑hour second set (B+). The pacing is solid with strong jams, though the opening half feels a bit tentative.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Underplays early numbers but adds energetic lead during jams and takes the melody on “China Cat”.
band_member_comments.Phil: Rhythm section slightly off in the first set; later provides driving focus on “Not Fade Away” and the Goin’ Down segment.
band_member_comments.Bob: Provides solid vocals, especially stepping into Bobby’s part on “China Cat”.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith Hartford shines with an arpeggio before “China Cat” and leads the dual‑drum climax on “Not Fade Away → Goin' Down”.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann delivers a standout performance, particularly in the tight “Not Fade Away > Goin’ Down the Road Feeling Bad” segment.
